videos.title: Discover Lagos in Nigeria with a Local
videos.description:
Lagos is not only Nigeria's largest city, it is also one of the largest metropolitan areas in Africa. This popular boomtown is the cultural and economic center of the West African country. Naturally, there is a lot to discover here! 
We have some travel tips for you straight from a local. Bibiresan Amodu (@bibiraii9887 ) shows you the best spots in Lagos. Check it out!

00:00 Intro
00:21 Lekki Conservation Centre
01:19 Landmark Beach
02:23 Amala Sky restaurant
03:36 Cera Cerni Art Hub
04:55 Enjoy the sunset by the water

CREDITS:
Report: Lusanda Chauke
Camera: Wemzi Akpan, Justice Mbui, David Chima
Editor: Chima Ojieh 
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Subscribe to DW Travel
youtube.com/dwtravel

Would you like to find out more about DW Travel? 
▸Website: dw.com/travel
▸Facebook: facebook.com/dw.travel
▸Instagram: instagram.com/dw_travel

DW Travel is showing you the captivating world of travel. Come along with us to regions and cities in Germany, throughout Europe and to far-away destinations.

#lagos #lagosnigeria #nigeria #africa #traveling
videos.transcript: This is not London Bridge. This is not the Amazon rainforest. This is not Hawaii. This is Lagos. Lagos is the economic heart of Nigeria and one of the fastest growing cities in Africa. Everything in Lagos is huge, especially the traffic jams. But did you know that in the center of the city, you can explore wildlife? You can be sure to spot a monkey. I'm literally about to walk on one of the longest canopy walks in all of Africa. From the top you can get amazing, unobstructed, 360-degree views of the city. The Lekki Conservation Center was built as a reserve sanctuary for the rich flora and fauna within the Lekki axis. It is home to the longest canopy walkway in Africa. And the center is definitely a breath of fresh air in the midst of the hustling and bustling city that is Lagos. Especially with its crazy traffic too. Here, there's something for everybody. With its canopy walk, boardwalk and tree houses it's ready for families and team buildings as well. If you are a seeker of adventure, this place is for you. Let's go to the next spot. So here I am walking alongside the beautiful beach that is Landmark Beach right here in Lagos. Landmark Beach is located on the beautiful shoreline of the Atlantic Ocean. Now a lot of people come here for its numerous activities, like volleyball. Okay guys, to win we have to come together. You take the left, you take the right, I'll be in the middle. In the middle of a hot day, you need something refreshing to cool you down. Hello. Hi. One coconut, please. Thank you. Cheers. That's it. Let's go. Landmark Beach is definitely a bucket list place to visit for every tourist in the city of Lagos. Or if you're looking at spending a day at the beach with family and friends. I am here at a local Nigerian spot that a lot of locals come to for an amazing dish called Amala. It's none other than Amala Sky. Let's go. Amala is made from yam or cassava flour, or sometimes unripe plantain flour. It's a traditional Nigerian dish served with ewedu, which is a classic Yoruba soup and often considered a swallow, which is starchy food or grains such as eba, pounded yam, or fufu. It already smells amazing, but you know what? I'll be the judge of that. Nigeria, being such a diverse country with various ethnic groups, the country offers a plethora of food. From the world famous jollof rice to banga soup and many more mouthwatering dishes. I love sharing fun facts with you guys. Here's a funny one about eating in Nigeria. No matter how full you are, you are never, and I mean never, too full to finish your meat. Well, that was a delicious meal. I don't chop belly full. What better way to sit back and relax and digest your food? And to engage the mind while painting. The culture of learning the art of painting has become a trendy activity in Lagos. Those that offered the activity focus on the fun and relaxation associated with the art and creativity. Hello! Hi! Hi, so nice to meet you! Nice to meet you! Welcome to Cera Cerni's Creative Hub! Thank you so much! So, what are we going to be doing today? Ok, the moment you step in here, you're an artist. I'm an artist! Bibi Basquiat, see! So just embrace the energy of the artist and let it show for us. So you can have your seat and bring out the Picasso in you. I told you already Bibi Basquiat now. Yes, Bibi Basquiat. We don't know what's gonna come of this, but you know what? It's never too late to give it a go. This must be one of the best ways to unwind. Art is therapeutic. Woah, Nah, I'm kidding. Wow, I have thoroughly enjoyed today. You've already seen all the things that I've gotten done in just one day. You can imagine how much more there is to do and see right here in Lagos. I even got the souvenirs to show it. Anyway, it's about that time that I show you what the sunset looks like in my city. Let's go. Lagos is known for its vibrant nightlife. And people love to party on this side of the equator. There are a number of bars, beachside bars, pubs and nightclubs, where one can enjoy a variety of music whilst having a drink. What better way to end the day than to sit back, relax, and enjoy a cocktail as you watch the sunset. Lagos City never dulls and there's always something to do whether it's the daytime or the nighttime. Cheers! It's been so much fun showing you my beautiful city of Lagos. Until next time, catch you guys on the flip side. Bye!

videos.transcript: welcome to Lagos Nigeria the most populated city in Africa they have a population of over 9 million people but I found something really interesting with a city that has that many people why is it that she never hear about the tourism in Lagos Nigeria if you know anything about Nigeria they're well known for their n life all the partying of course afro beats but we all know Nigeria loves to party but why don't we hear anything about their tourism and all the cool and beautiful things to do in see out there but if we can be honest traveling to Nigeria as an American citizen like myself it is not the easiest due to their complicated Visa process also traveling into Lagos can be really stressful CU if you don't know about Lagos Airport it's probably known to be one of the most interesting airports in the world but I took a different route getting into Nigeria I drove from the country of Bene Republic and if I didn't have a local guide with me the journey might have not been as smooth but it worked out in this video I'm going to share with you the top 10 things to do in Legos Nigeria outside of partying and nighti number one where to stay in Legos Nigeria where you stay in Legos is really going to make or break your trip I stayed at a very good and convenient hotel by name of white orchid hotel located in Victoria Island Victoria Island is one of the most developed and safest areas in Legos with top tier restaurants luxury shopping centers nightlife spots and enter tment venues staying in this area puts you right in the center of all the action making it super easy to explore the city which is really what you want what makes white orchid Hotel Stand Out is that it's more than just a place to sleep it's an entire experience this Boutique Hotel features beautifully designed rooms with a mix of modern and Nigerian Aesthetics check to the hotel now it's time to see the room so first we have this little round chair area here with a table you can sit sip on some teeth wash some TV then you have the bed which is extremely comfortable here um this right here is one of my favorite Parts this room come on check out here on the nightstand you have like this Old School record player that actually plays right you can play some records I it's crazy they got Ray Charles true to life never heard that but probably going to give it a try the service is top- Notch and breakfast is an experience on its own featuring a variety of fresh and local Nigerian Cuisine and what sets this hotel apart is because it is attached and a part of the ebony life Resort so it's literally right next door so this gives you access to multiple rooftop restaurants and bars with stunning views of the Lego Skyline another reason why I like this hotel/ Resort is because they also have luxury movie theater which is actually one of the most popular movie theaters in West Africa you also get access to a variety of entertainment options perfect for a fun night out without even having to leave your hotel grounds so whether you're just looking to to chill at your hotel and just you know hang out handle some business you can also spend some time at the resort get access to all these different restaurants you know the movie theater there's a nice pool so I feel like it's the best of both worlds staying at this property number two kaying this is one of the best outdoor adventure experiences that you can have in Legos I was actually pretty surprised to see kaying in Legos so you go kayaking in Ley Phase 1 this is actually an upscale part of Legos known for its modern development and beautiful Waterfront kayaking here gives you a chance to padd along the coast while taking in the amazing views of the Lego Skyline this experience is peaceful yet thrilling as you navigate through Lego's Coastal Waters and the best part is beginner friendly so even if you've never went kayaking before you can still enjoy it there are guys available to help you and you can rent a single or double kayaking depending on whether you want to go solo or if you want to go with a group going kayaking in Legos was easily one of my favorite parts of the trip this a perfect activity if you want to get away from all the hustle and bustle of Legos something peaceful on the water with an amazing view I highly encourage this experience this was easily one of my favorite things to do while being Legos Nigeria number three TAA Bay Beach now Legos has some amazing and beautiful beaches and T Bay Beach is easily one of the most popular beaches in Lagos now you would probably assume that it being is the most popular Beach is probably noisy crowd Ed but it's actually not it's actually a really laid-back and peaceful Beach that's worth visiting this beach is only accessible by boat and the ride itself is an experience you'll get to see the Legos Coastline and even pass by some historic landmarks along the way once you arrive you'll find soft and Sandy Shores clean water and it's perfect for swimming jet ski rentals and other water sports for those looking for excitement local vendors selling fresh coconuts and delicious Seafood I was able to get myself some really nice handmade pants and short sets that you can only find in Legos Nigeria whether you want to chill on the beach go swimming or even do some water activities Taha Bay Beach is the perfect place to spend the day number four Lighthouse Beach if you want a more secluded and non toury Beach that really a lot of people don't even know about Lighthouse Beach is probably for you this is one of the cleanest beaches in lakos and most Travelers don't even know about it unlike other beaches that can get crowded Lighthouse Beach offers a more peaceful and private experience here's what makes it special the white sandy beaches Clear Blue Waters Perfect for photos and relaxation a quiet Serene atmosphere making one of the best hidden gems in my opinion in Legos and it's also an affordable way to enjoy the day at the beach without spending a lot and one of my personal favorite things to do at Lighthouse Beach was really jumping on some ATVs and just driving up and down all across the beach it gave you such an amazing opportunity to just really bask in The Amazing views while having that that exhilarating fun on the ATVs if you want a beach experience that feels like an Escape From the City Lighthouse Beach is a perfect choice number five walk the longest canopy walkway in Africa at Ley Conservation Center this is hands down one of the most popular attractions here in Lagos Nigeria if not Nigeria this is a series of high bridges that take you through the Treetops offering stunning aerial views of the forest below you'll get to experience Wildlife like monkeys exotic birds and even crocodiles there's a 95-year-old tortoise which is one of the oldest in Nigeria a massive outdoor chess board where you can play a life-size game this is one of the best places in Legos to enjoy nature adventure and a little bit of wildlife all in one number six experience dinner and entertainment at the library if you're looking for a dinner experience with a Twist the library is a good spot to check out this isn't just a restaurant it's a mix of dining and live entertainment as you enjoy your meal you'll be treated to world class performances from live music to cultural dance shows and once the dinner's over the place transforms into a lively nightlife spot with drinks music and dancing if you're looking for a night out that blends in amazing food and entertainment the library is a great spot I had the salmon with some gelof rice and it was just an allaround Vibe I'm not really a party person I went more so for the dinner and the entertainment portion which is honestly all the night like I personally needed it was a Vibe number seven find dining at Knock by Alara this is is known to be a place to get some of the best cuisine in Lagos Nigeria this restaurant serves a mixture of African cuisine with a modern twist featuring dishes from different parts of the continent the flavors are bold the presentation is stunning if you're looking to experience the best of African cuisine in a fine dining setting this is a perfect place for you everything that I ate at this restaurant was amazing even the drinks were good number eight visit nay Art Gallery the largest Art Gallery in all of West Africa for our lovers cultural enthusiasts and anyone who appreciates creativity a visit to Nick Art Gallery is an absolute must when visiting Legos this five-story building is the largest Art Gallery in West Africa housing over 25,000 art pieces created by talented local Nigerian artists from traditional African paintings to carved sculptures every floor of this Gallery is filled with stunning artwork that tells the rich story of Nigeria's history culture and traditions I even got the chance to meet the founder of the gallery which is nay she's a world-renowned Nigerian artist textile designer and cultural Ambassador she's such a warm and inspiring person full of wisdom and so much knowledge it was an honor to meet someone who has dedicated her life to uplifting the African art and culture if you love art you'll love the niay art gallery also while being at this Art Gallery they gave me the opportunity to try on some local Nigerian garments and also they gave me my Nigerian name listen guys listen my name sorry okay create ground yes my name is t IO okay I'm from the euroba tribe number nine explore makoko aka the Venice of Africa makoko is easily one of the most interesting and unique places in Lagos it's a floating village where thousands of people live in houses built on wooden stils above the water the entire Community depends on canoes for Trans a making it a world completely different from the busy streets of Lagos one of the best ways to experience Moco is by taking a guided tour as you glide through the water you'll see locals paddling canoes children playing and fishermen going about their daily work the tour gives you the chance to meet residents learn about their culture and understand how theyve built a life in a unique environment mcoo is a Place full of resilience and history despite the challenges of living on water the people have created a tightened Community with schools churches and markets all operating within the floating village visiting here is a humbling and eye opening experience that shows a side of Legos most tourists never see if you do plan to visit makoko I highly encourage you to go with a local guide number one so that way you can feel safe but also so that way you can really travel someone that's knowledgeable that could teach you about the culture and really what's happening in that Village number 10 go shopping by market literally one of the the biggest markets here in Lagos Nigeria come on side we're about to we're about to see what it's all about what they got they sell they sell everything so come on this Market's a little hectic but it's an experience that you don't want to miss this is the largest Open Air Market in Nigeria covering multiple streets in Legos Island and offering a wild energetic shopping experience like no other here you can find everything from Fabrics jewelry designer knockoffs to electronics household goods and souvenirs it's the perfect place to shop for unique nerian fashion or grab a few authentic West African fabrics to take home however be prepared for the hustle and bustle bu gun Market is crowded loud and full of life sellers will be calling you to check out their goods bargaining is a must and if you're good at negotiating this is where you want to use those negotiating skills but I'm going to be honest with you this is a place that you really want to be with a local because if they know that you're not from there I'm trying to figure out the best way to say this just know they'll they'll smell blood in the water okay so it's best to to really be with the logo my favorite thing to do at this Market was to try out the fresh sugar cane my man was chopping it up right there on the street I had me a whole bag and it was absolutely amazing I was eating it for the rest of the day even if you're not buying anything or looking to buy anything I would highly encourage you visit this Market because you get the opportunity to experience the energy and culture of Lagos and this is why you should visit Lagos Nigeria if you enjoy this video hit the like button also let me know in the comments if you plan on going to Legos Nigeria if you've been to Legos Nigeria or perhaps you are from Nigeria let me know in the comments if you want to see more content like this make sure you hit videos.transcript: Airports everywhere can be stressful and Lagos National Airport is no different. With the crowds, the rush, the chaos of travel, it's no surprise that a few people try to take advantage of travelers who don't know any better. Even smart, experienced travelers sometimes get caught out. That's why in this video, I'm breaking down the five biggest scams at Lagos International Airport. The ones people fall for every single day. so you can travel with confidence and avoid unnecessary stress. If you come to Nigeria for Dead to December or you are traveling out of Nigeria for the very first time, you need to see this. And if you're new here, hi, I'm Sassy from Kev. I help Nigerians and Africans around the world travel smarter, cheaper, and with less wahala. So, make sure you hit that subscribe button so you never miss my airport survival guides. Scam one, the trolley scam. In many airports, trolleys are free. But in Lagos, it's a mixed bag. At the new terminal, some are free. At the old terminal, you must pay. But here comes the scam. Random guys claiming a trolley is 5k, 5,000 naira. Lies. The official rate is usually between 500 naira and 5,000 naira max depending on the size and whether you hire a porter to help you. But if you don't know, you play like you're buying the trolley to take home. Always head straight to the official trolley desk or airport staff. Don't price trolley like you're shopping for Pepe at Mile 12 Market. I'll put the official trolley pricing as at May 2025 up on screen now. Scout the currency exchange sharks. You want to change dollars to naira. That's when the whisper starts. Madame, I'll give you a better rate. Tempting, right? until you realize you've been shortch changed or worse, you're holding fake notes. This usually happens outside of the airport building where men stand around flashing bundles of naira to lure travelers. Don't be fooled. These hustlers can turn $100 into monopoly money before you even leave arrivals. If you must change money, use the official bur they change inside of the airport. And honestly, if you can wait until in town, that's even better. but outside the airport building. Don't try it. Scam three, the prohibited items fee. This one can frustrate you. Officials check your bag and suddenly say, "Madame, you're carrying prohibited items. You must pay a fee." Sometimes, yes, items like fresh meat, fruits, or lack liquids are restricted depending on your destination, but other times it's just intimidation. The best way to beat this, arm yourself with knowledge. Know what's actually prohibited for your airline and destination country. That way, if they try it, you can confidently say, "No, sir, that item is allowed." Tell me, have you experienced this scam before? Scam number four, pay or miss your flight. This one is pure intimidation. It mostly targets firsttime travelers. I've had people DM me saying officials at the airport told them if you don't settle, we'll delay you until your flight leaves. Can you imagine? Here's my advice. Don't panic. Politely ask what exactly am I paying for? If you can record the interaction and then say, "Please, I want to speak to your supervisor." Scammers hate supervisors. Once they know you're not an easy target, they usually back off. Never hand over money in fear. That just foils the whole scam. Scam five, fake helpers. And finally, the scam almost everyone complains about, fake helpers. You arrive in Lagos and before you can even say, "Thank God, five guys rush at you. Madam, let me help you." Bags are grabbed, trolley rushed, all before you can even react. Some are just hoping for tips, but many will assist you and then demand outrageous payment later. Even if they flash an ID, just know this and know peace. At Lagos airport, no help is free. One of my friends told that she gave them a,000 NRA once and the person looked in disgust and was like, "What is this?" My personal trick, I simply tell them, "Sure, you can help me. Oh, but I have no money to give you." the speed at which they drop that trolley. Olympic level sprints. This is the number one scam travelers complain about. And once you've experienced it, you will never forget it. And there you have it. The five scam travelers fall for at Lagos International Airport. If you've ever experienced any of these, share your stories in the comments below. I would love to hear them. And don't forget to hit subscribe for more Lagos airport guides. The more you know, the less stress and wala you'll face when you travel. Thank you so much for watching and I'll see you in the next one.
